do_not_overweight: Treat these as priors, not recipes.
Stay inside the current baseline family unless measurements justify a bigger change.
external_priors: Top public competitors often improve the baseline family rather than replacing it.
Wider MLPs, often around 3x, appear frequently in strong runs.
Mixed lower-bit quantization on large matrices appears promising.
FP16 tied embedding passthrough appears promising.
Lower LR, higher Muon momentum, and longer warmdown appear promising.
Sliding-window evaluation appears in strong public runs, but only use it if it stays competition-valid.
next_priority_axes: lower-bit or mixed-bit matrix quantization feasibility
optimizer schedule: lower LR / higher Muon momentum / longer warmdown

disallowed_micro_axes: single-constant edits without a larger hypothesis
temporary_mode.goal: Try a small number of larger, leaderboard-informed jumps before H100 rehearsal.
temporary_mode.name: structural_jump_phase
priority_axes: mlp_mult with materially wider MLP, including ~3x if feasible
fp16 tied embedding or embedding/head precision passthrough
optimizer schedule retune as a coherent bundle: lower LR, higher Muon momentum, longer warmdown
int6 or mixed low-bit quantization on large matrices if it can be implemented cleanly
sliding-window evaluation only if it remains competition-valid
seq_len toward 2048 if feasible under the budget
rules: Prefer substantial changes over neighborhood hill-climbing.
Do not use this phase for tiny LR-only, clip-only, adapter-alpha-only, or hidden-bonus-only tweaks.
Each experiment should test a major hypothesis with plausible upside beyond noise.
If no meaningful win appears after max_iterations, return to the accepted best and resume normal search.
